title: The utility of casein skin prick test and IgE values in predicting anaphylaxis and reactivity to baked milk.
author_string: Cogurlu, Mujde Tuba, Uluc, Nezihe Nefise, Ozanli, Ismail, Ozkan, Yeşim Ece, Iskender, Nagihan et al.
journal_full: Annals of allergy, asthma & immunology : official publication of the American College of Allergy, Asthma, & Immunology
abstract: Most children with milk allergy can tolerate baked milk (BM); however, a small percent still react to it. Identifying indicators that might forecast potential reactions to baked goods is essential. To determine the predictive factors and some decision points for estimation of BM reactivity and severe reactions. A cross-sectional study was performed. Participants who were reactive to unheated milk underwent an oral food challenge with BM. The reactive group was classified into anaphylactic and non-anaphylactic reaction categories. The IgE and skin prick test (SPT) levels of milk and its components were compared among these groups. The study was conducted with 110 patients. The median age of participants was 16 (IQR: 12-31) months. BM reactivity was observed in 41% (n = 46/110) of the patients. The optimal cutoff point for BM reactivity was 4.68 kIU/L for casein specific IgE (sIgE) (84% specificity, 75% sensitivity, area under the curve [AUC]: 0.827) and 7 mm for casein SPT (87.2% specificity, 51.1% sensitivity, AUC: 0.721) (P < .001, P < .001). The positive decision point for anaphylaxis for casein sIgE was 34 kIU/L (95% specificity, 47.6% sensitivity, AUC: 0.707). In the group younger than 24 months old, the cutoff values for casein sIgE were found to be 5.4 kIU/L (95% specificity, 66.6% sensitivity, AUC: 0.844) and casein SPT at 7 mm (91% specificity, 37% sensitivity, AUC: 0.708) (P < .001, P = .002). Our investigation revealed that the most accurate indicator for BM reactivity was casein sIgE and SPT. Although we established a threshold for severe reaction with BM in our study population, we consider that our findings require validation before therapeutic implementation.
